## Callbacks

All callbacks are optional. Define any in `main.lua` and the engine calls them automatically.

```lua
function luna.load()  -- Called once after the script is loaded.
function luna.update( dt : number )  -- Called every frame; dt = elapsed seconds.
function luna.draw()  -- Called every frame for rendering.
function luna.keypressed( key : string, scancode : string, isrepeat : boolean )  -- Key press event.
function luna.keyreleased( key : string, scancode : string )  -- Key release event.
function luna.textinput( text : string )  -- Unicode character typed.
function luna.mousepressed( x : number, y : number, button : number )  -- Mouse button press.
function luna.mousereleased( x : number, y : number, button : number )  -- Mouse button release.
function luna.wheelmoved( x : number, y : number )  -- Mouse wheel scroll.
function luna.gamepadpressed( id : number, button : string )  -- Gamepad button press.
function luna.gamepadreleased( id : number, button : string )  -- Gamepad button release.
function luna.gamepadaxis( id : number, axis : string, value : number )  -- Gamepad axis; value in -1..1.
function luna.joystickadded( id : number )  -- Gamepad connected.
function luna.joystickremoved( id : number )  -- Gamepad disconnected.
function luna.touchpressed( id, x : number, y : number, dx : number, dy : number, pressure : number )  -- Touch begin.
function luna.touchmoved(  id, x : number, y : number, dx : number, dy : number, pressure : number )  -- Touch move.
function luna.touchreleased(id, x : number, y : number, dx : number, dy : number, pressure : number )  -- Touch end.
function luna.focus( focused : boolean )  -- Window focus change.
function luna.visible( visible : boolean )  -- Window show/hide.
function luna.resize( w : number, h : number )  -- Window resized.
function luna.quit()  -- Return true to cancel quit.
function luna.errorhandler( msg : string )  -- Unhandled Lua error.
```

### `Canvas`

Lua UserData wrapper for an off-screen canvas resource.

```lua
Canvas:getDimensions()  -- Returns the canvas width and height in pixels
Canvas:getHeight() -> integer  -- Returns the canvas height in pixels
Canvas:getWidth() -> integer  -- Returns the canvas width in pixels
```

### `Font`

Lua UserData wrapper for a loaded font resource.

```lua
Font:getAscent() -> number  -- Returns the ascent (distance from baseline to the top of the tallest glyph) in pixels
Font:getDescent() -> number  -- Returns the descent (distance below the baseline for descenders) in pixels
Font:getHeight() -> integer  -- Returns the line height of this font at its loaded size, in pixels
Font:getLineHeight()  -- Returns the line height in pixels used when advancing to the next line of text
Font:getWidth() -> number  -- Measures the rendered width of `text` using this font's current size
Font:setLineHeight()  -- Sets the line height multiplier used when laying out multi-line text
```

### `Image`

Lua UserData wrapper for a loaded texture resource.

```lua
Image:getDimensions()  -- Returns image width and height in pixels
Image:getFilter()  -- Returns the current min and mag texture filters
Image:getHeight() -> integer  -- Returns the image height in pixels
Image:getWidth() -> integer  -- Returns the image width in pixels
Image:getWrap()  -- Returns the current texture wrap mode for the horizontal and vertical axes
```

### `NineSlice`

Lua UserData wrapper for a nine-slice (9-patch) image definition.

```lua
NineSlice:draw()  -- Draws to the current render target
NineSlice:getInsets()  -- Returns the insets
NineSlice:getTextureSize()  -- Returns the texture size
```

### `Shape`

```lua
Shape:circle()  -- Appends a circle to this shape
Shape:clear()  -- Clears all commands from this shape and resets color and line-width state
Shape:getCommandCount() -> integer  -- Returns the number of draw commands currently queued in this shape
Shape:line()  -- Appends a single line segment to this shape
Shape:polygon()  -- Appends a polygon to this shape from a flat vararg vertex list
Shape:polyline()  -- Appends a polyline to this shape from a flat vararg point list
Shape:setColor()  -- Sets the active draw color for subsequent commands in this shape
Shape:setLineWidth()  -- Sets the stroke width for outlined primitives that follow in this shape
```

### `SpriteBatch`

Lua UserData wrapper for a sprite batch resource.

```lua
SpriteBatch:clear()  -- Removes all sprites from this batch and resets the sprite count to zero
SpriteBatch:getBufferSize()  -- Returns the maximum number of sprites this batch was allocated to hold
SpriteBatch:getCount()  -- Returns the number of sprites currently added to this batch
```

## `luna.window` {#window}

> Window Api implementation for the `lua_api` subsystem.
>
> This module is part of Luna2D's `lua_api` subsystem and provides the implementation
> details for window api-related operations and data management.

## `luna.timer` {#timer}

> Timer Api implementation for the `lua_api` subsystem.
>
> This module is part of Luna2D's `lua_api` subsystem and provides the implementation
> details for timer api-related operations and data management.
> Primary functions: `register()`.

*Coverage: 25/25 items documented (100%)*

```lua
luna.timer.getAverageDelta()  -- Returns the rolling-average frame delta time in seconds
luna.timer.getDelta()  -- Returns the delta time (seconds) for the current frame
luna.timer.getFPS()  -- Returns the measured frames-per-second for the current frame
luna.timer.getMicroTime()  -- Returns the high-resolution monotonic timer value in microseconds
luna.timer.getTime()  -- Returns the total elapsed time in seconds since engine start
luna.timer.newScheduler()  -- Creates an independent timer scheduler for managing a set of callbacks
luna.timer.sleep( seconds : number )  -- Suspends execution for the given number of seconds
luna.timer.step()  -- Advances the timer by one step (called automatically each frame)
```

### `mlua`

```lua
mlua:after( delay : number, func : function )  -- Calls a Lua function after the given delay in seconds
mlua:cancel( id : integer )  -- Cancels a scheduled timer callback
mlua:cancelAll()  -- Cancels and removes every pending timer entry from this scheduler
mlua:cancelNamed( name : string ) -> boolean  -- Cancels a specific named timer that was scheduled on this scheduler
mlua:getCount() -> integer  -- Returns the number of active timer entries currently in the scheduler
mlua:getInterval( id : integer )  -- Returns the repeat interval of the given timer entry in seconds
mlua:getRemaining( id : integer )  -- Returns the time remaining before the next invocation of the given timer
mlua:getRepeatCount( id : integer )  -- Returns how many times the given timer has fired since it was created
mlua:getTimeScale()  -- Returns the current time-scale factor applied to all timers in this scheduler
mlua:isEmpty() -> boolean  -- Returns whether the scheduler has no active timer entries
mlua:isPaused( id : integer )  -- Returns whether the scheduler is currently paused and not advancing timers
mlua:pause( id : integer )  -- Pauses the scheduler so no pending callbacks fire until resumed
mlua:resetEvent( id : integer )  -- Resets the elapsed time of the given timer so it fires again after its full interval
mlua:resume( id : integer )  -- Resumes a paused scheduler, allowing its callbacks to fire again
mlua:setInterval( id : integer, interval : number )  -- Changes the repeat interval of an existing timer without recreating it
mlua:setTimeScale( scale : number )  -- Sets a time-scale factor that speeds up or slows down all timers in the scheduler
mlua:update( dt : number )  -- Advances all pending timers by dt seconds
```

## `luna.audio` {#audio}

> Audio Api implementation for the `lua_api` subsystem.
>
> This module is part of Luna2D's `lua_api` subsystem and provides the implementation
> details for audio api-related operations and data management.
> Key types exported from this module: `LuaSource`, `LuaBus`, `LuaMidiPlayer`.
> Primary functions: `register()`.

### `Bus`

Lua UserData wrapper for an audio bus. Consult the module-level documentation for the broader usage context and preconditions.

```lua
Bus:getName()  -- Returns the name of this audio bus
Bus:getPitch()  -- Returns the pitch multiplier of this bus
Bus:getVolume()  -- Returns the current volume scale of this audio mixer bus
Bus:isPaused()  -- Returns whether this bus is currently paused
Bus:pause()  -- Pauses all audio sources that are currently playing through this bus
Bus:resume()  -- Resumes all paused sources on this bus
Bus:setPitch( pitch : number )  -- Sets the pitch multiplier for all sources on this bus
Bus:setVolume( vol : number )  -- Sets the volume for all sources routed to this bus
```


```lua
Source:resume()  -- Resumes playback of this audio source from its current paused position
Source:seek( pos : number )  -- Seeks playback to `offset` seconds from the start
Source:setHighpass( cutoff_hz : integer )  -- Applies a high-pass filter to the audio source
Source:setLooping( looping : boolean )  -- Enables or disables looping. When enabled, the source restarts automatically when it reaches the end
Source:setLowpass( cutoff_hz : integer )  -- Applies a low-pass filter to the audio source
Source:setPan( pan : number )  -- Sets the stereo panning (-1.0 left to 1.0 right) of the source
Source:setPitch( pitch : number )  -- Sets the playback pitch multiplier. `1.0` is normal pitch; `2.0` doubles frequency
Source:setVolume( vol : number )  -- Sets playback volume. `1.0` is full volume; `0.0` is silent
Source:stop()  -- Stops playback and resets the seek position to the beginning
Source:tell()  -- Returns the current playback position in seconds
```

## `luna.physics` {#physics}

> Registers the `luna.physics.*` physics API.

*Coverage: 109/109 items documented (100%)*

```lua
luna.physics.addDistanceJoint()  -- Adds a distance joint that maintains a fixed separation between two body anchor points
luna.physics.addFrictionJoint()  -- Adds a friction joint that resists relative movement and rotation between two bodies
luna.physics.addGearJoint()  -- Adds a gear joint that couples two revolute or prismatic joints so they move in sync
luna.physics.addMotorJoint()  -- Adds a motor joint that drives one body toward a target position relative to another
luna.physics.addMouseJoint()  -- Adds a mouse joint that applies a spring force pulling a body toward a target point
luna.physics.addPrismaticJoint()  -- Adds a prismatic joint that constrains two bodies to slide along a single axis
luna.physics.addPulleyJoint()  -- Adds a pulley joint linking two bodies through a rope of fixed total length
luna.physics.addRevoluteJoint()  -- Adds a revolute joint that constrains two bodies to rotate around a shared anchor point
luna.physics.addRopeJoint()  -- Adds a rope joint that enforces a maximum distance between two body anchor points
luna.physics.addWeldJoint()  -- Adds a weld joint that rigidly fixes the relative position and angle of two bodies
luna.physics.addWheelJoint()  -- Adds a wheel joint combining a revolute motor and a prismatic spring for vehicle suspension
luna.physics.applyAngularImpulse()  -- Applies an instantaneous angular impulse to the body, changing its spin velocity
luna.physics.applyForce()  -- Applies a force vector to a body at its center of mass
luna.physics.applyForceAtPoint()  -- Applies a world-space force at a given point on the body, potentially adding torque
luna.physics.applyImpulse()  -- Applies an instantaneous impulse to a body
luna.physics.applyTorque()  -- Applies a rotational torque to a body
luna.physics.attachShape()  -- Attaches a standalone shape to a body as a new collider fixture
luna.physics.destroyBody()  -- Removes a body from the physics world
luna.physics.destroyJoint()  -- Removes the given joint from the world and frees its resources
luna.physics.destroyWorld()  -- Destroys a physics world and frees all associated resources
luna.physics.getAngularDamping()  -- Returns the angular damping coefficient of the body that slows its rotation over time
luna.physics.getAngularVelocity()  -- Returns the current angular velocity of the body in radians per second
luna.physics.getBodies()  -- Returns a table of all body IDs currently registered in the physics world
luna.physics.getBody()  -- Returns the body object table for the given body ID within the world
luna.physics.getBodyAngle()  -- Returns the current rotation angle (radians) of a physics body
luna.physics.getBodyAtPoint() -> Body  -- Returns the first body whose collider contains the given world-space point
luna.physics.getBodyContacts()  -- Returns all active contact manifolds where this body touches another body
luna.physics.getBodyCount()  -- Returns the number of bodies in the world
luna.physics.getBodyMass()  -- Returns the mass of a physics body
luna.physics.getBodyType()  -- Returns the type string of the given body
luna.physics.getCollisions()  -- Returns all collision pairs currently overlapping in the physics world
luna.physics.getContacts()  -- Returns a table of all currently active collision contact manifolds in the world
luna.physics.getGravity()  -- Returns the global gravity vector (gx, gy)
luna.physics.getGravityScale()  -- Returns the per-body gravity scale factor applied to the world gravity vector
luna.physics.getJointBodies()  -- Returns the two body IDs connected by the given joint
luna.physics.getJointCount()  -- Returns the total number of joints currently active in the physics world
luna.physics.getJointLimits()  -- Returns the lower and upper angular or linear limits set on the joint
luna.physics.getJointMotorSpeed()  -- Returns the target motor speed of a revolute or prismatic joint
luna.physics.getJointType()  -- Returns a string describing the type of the given joint
luna.physics.getJoints()  -- Returns a table of all joint IDs currently active in the physics world
luna.physics.getLinearDamping()  -- Returns the linear damping coefficient that gradually slows the body's movement
luna.physics.getMeter() -> Pixels  -- Returns the current pixels-per-meter scale set on the physics world
luna.physics.isBullet()  -- Returns whether continuous collision detection (CCD) is enabled on the body
luna.physics.isFixedRotation()  -- Returns whether the body's rotation is locked and prevented from changing
luna.physics.isSleepingAllowed()  -- Returns whether the body is allowed to enter a sleep state when inactive
luna.physics.newBody()  -- Creates a rigid body in the given world
luna.physics.newChainBody()  -- Creates a static chain body from a list of connected edge segments
luna.physics.newChainShape() -> Shape  -- Creates a standalone chain shape from a closed/open flag followed by flat vertex coordinates
luna.physics.newCircleBody()  -- Creates a circle-shaped physics body at the given world position
luna.physics.newCircleShape() -> Shape  -- Creates a standalone circle shape
luna.physics.newEdgeBody()  -- Creates a single-segment edge body connecting two points in world space
luna.physics.newEdgeShape() -> Shape  -- Creates a standalone line-segment shape
luna.physics.newPolygonBody()  -- Creates a convex polygon body defined by a list of local-space vertices
luna.physics.newPolygonShape() -> Shape  -- Creates a standalone convex polygon shape from flat vertex coordinates
luna.physics.newRectangleShape() -> Shape  -- Creates a standalone rectangle shape from half-extents
luna.physics.newRegularPolygonBody()  -- Creates a new regular polygon body
luna.physics.newWorld()  -- Creates a new physics simulation world
luna.physics.queryBoundingBox()  -- Returns all bodies whose axis-aligned bounding boxes overlap the given query rectangle
luna.physics.raycast()  -- Casts a ray from the origin in the given direction and returns the first body hit
luna.physics.raycastAll()  -- Casts a ray and returns every body it intersects, sorted by distance
luna.physics.setAngularDamping()  -- Sets the angular damping coefficient that gradually reduces the body's spin
luna.physics.setAngularVelocity()  -- Sets the angular velocity of the body to the given value in radians per second
luna.physics.setBodyAngle()  -- Sets the rotation angle (radians) of a physics body
luna.physics.setBodyMass()  -- Overrides the simulated mass of the body with the given value
luna.physics.setBodyPosition()  -- Sets the position of a physics body
luna.physics.setBodySize()  -- Sets the width and height dimensions of the body's collision shape
luna.physics.setBodyType()  -- Sets the body type: 'dynamic', 'static', or 'kinematic'
luna.physics.setBodyVelocity()  -- Sets the linear velocity of a physics body
luna.physics.setBullet()  -- Enables continuous collision detection (CCD) on a body to prevent tunneling at high speeds
luna.physics.setFixedRotation()  -- Locks or unlocks the body's rotation axis so it cannot spin in the simulation
luna.physics.setGravity()  -- Sets the global gravity vector for the world
luna.physics.setGravityScale()  -- Sets a per-body multiplier applied to the world gravity vector for this body
luna.physics.setJointLimits()  -- Sets the lower and upper angular or linear limits on a constrained joint
luna.physics.setJointLimitsEnabled()  -- Enables or disables the angular/linear limit constraints on a joint
luna.physics.setJointMotorSpeed()  -- Sets the target motor speed for a motorized revolute or prismatic joint
luna.physics.setLinearDamping()  -- Sets the linear damping coefficient that gradually slows the body's movement
luna.physics.setMeter()  -- Sets the pixels-per-meter ratio used to convert physics world units to screen pixels
luna.physics.setMouseJointTarget()  -- Updates the target world-space point that the mouse joint pulls the body toward
luna.physics.setSleepingAllowed()  -- Sets whether the body is allowed to enter a sleep state when it comes to rest
luna.physics.step()  -- Advances the physics simulation by the given timestep in seconds
```

### `Body`

Lua UserData wrapper for a physics body.

```lua
Body:addFixture()  -- Adds a collision fixture to the body using the given shape definition table
Body:applyForce()  -- Applies a continuous force (accumulates until the next physics step) to the body's centre of mass
Body:applyImpulse()  -- Applies an instantaneous impulse directly to the body's centre of mass
Body:destroy()  -- Destroys the body and removes it from its parent physics world immediately
Body:getAngle() -> number  -- Returns the body's current rotation angle in radians
Body:getBodyType()  -- Returns the type string of the given body
Body:getFixtureCount()  -- Returns the number of collision fixtures currently attached to the body
Body:getMass()  -- Returns the total simulated mass of the body in physics units
Body:getPosition()  -- Returns the body's current world-space position
Body:getVelocity()  -- Returns the current linear velocity vector of the body
Body:setAngle()  -- Sets the body's rotation to `angle` radians (bypasses physics)
Body:setPosition()  -- Teleports the body to the given world-space position (bypasses collision detection)
Body:setSize()  -- Resizes the body's primary collision shape to the given width and height
Body:setVelocity()  -- Sets the body's linear velocity to the given (vx, vy) world-space vector
```

### `Shape`

Lua UserData wrapper for a standalone physics shape.

```lua
Shape:destroy()  -- Destroys the shape. No-op — Lua GC handles cleanup automatically
Shape:getBoundingBox()  -- Returns the axis-aligned bounding box of this shape in local coordinates
Shape:getRadius() -> number  -- Returns the radius of a circle shape
Shape:getType() -> string  -- Returns the shape type string: `"circle"`, `"rectangle"`, `"polygon"`, `"edge"`, or `"chain"`
Shape:setDensity()  -- Sets the density for when this shape is attached to a body
Shape:setFriction()  -- Sets the friction coefficient for when this shape is attached to a body
Shape:setRestitution()  -- Sets the restitution (bounciness) for when this shape is attached to a body
Shape:setSensor()  -- Sets whether this shape acts as a sensor (detects overlaps, no forces)
```

### `World`

Lua UserData wrapper for a physics world.

```lua
World:getBodyCount()  -- Returns the number of bodies in the world
World:getCollisions()  -- Returns a table of all currently active collision pairs in this physics world
World:getGravity()  -- Returns the current world gravity vector
World:isSleepingAllowed()  -- Returns whether bodies in this world are allowed to enter a sleep state
World:setCallbacks()  -- Registers collision begin/end callback functions for this physics world
World:setGravity()  -- Sets world gravity. Default is `(0, 9.81)` (downward)
World:step()  -- Advances the physics simulation by `dt` seconds, resolving collisions and integrating forces
```

```lua
luna.filesystem.read( path : string )  -- Reads a text file and returns its contents as a string
```

> Event Api implementation for the `lua_api` subsystem.

> details for event api-related operations and data management.

```lua

```

## `luna.data` {#data}

> Registers the `luna.data.*` LÖVE2D-compatible binary data API.
>
> Provides the Lua-side `luna.data` namespace with functions that mirror the
> LÖVE2D `love.data.*` surface: `pack`, `unpack`, `getPackedSize`,
> `compress`, `decompress`, `encode`, `decode`, `hash`, `newByteData`,
> and `newDataView`.

*Coverage: 15/24 items documented (62%)*

```lua
luna.data.compress( format_str : string, raw_data : string, level : integer? )  -- Compresses data using the given algorithm (''deflate'', ''gzip'', ''lz4'')
luna.data.decode( format_str : string, encoded : string )  -- Decodes encoded text back to binary using the given format (''base64'', ''hex'')
luna.data.decompress( format_str : string, compressed_data : string )  -- Decompresses data using the given algorithm (''deflate'', ''gzip'', ''lz4'')
luna.data.encode( format_str : string, raw_data : string )  -- Encodes binary data using the given format (''base64'', ''hex'')
luna.data.encodeToml( tbl : table )  -- Encodes a Lua table as a TOML string
luna.data.getPackedSize( format : string )  -- Returns the number of bytes the given format and values would occupy
luna.data.hash( algo_str : string, raw_data : string )  -- Returns the cryptographic hash (md5, sha1, sha256, sha512) of the input
luna.data.newByteData( value : any )  -- Creates a new mutable byte buffer of the given size or from a string
luna.data.newDataView( data : string, offset : integer?, size : integer? ) -> DataView  -- Creates a read-only windowed view into a byte string
luna.data.pack( format : string )  -- Packs values into a binary byte string using the LÖVE2D format string
luna.data.parseToml( toml_string : string )  -- Parses a TOML string and returns a Lua table representation
luna.data.read( format : string, data : string, offset : integer? )  -- Reads values using the Luna2D Binary Pack Format
luna.data.size( format : string ) -> integer  -- Returns the byte size of a Luna2D Binary Pack Format string
luna.data.unpack( format : string, raw_data : string, offset : integer? )  -- Unpacks values from a LÖVE2D-format binary byte string
luna.data.write( format : string )  -- Writes values using the Luna2D Binary Pack Format (space-separated type tokens)
```

### `DataView`

```lua
DataView:getSize()

```

```lua
Universe:bitmapUntag( id : integer, name : string )  -- Bitmap untag on this Universe

Universe:getEntitiesByTag( tag : string )  -- Returns the entities by tag

Universe:getSystemCount()  -- Returns the system count
Universe:getTags( id : integer )  -- Returns the tags
Universe:has( id : integer, name : string ) -> boolean  -- Returns `true` if the condition is met
Universe:hasBitmapTag( id : integer, name : string )  -- Returns `true` if bitmap tag
Universe:hasBlueprint( name : string ) -> boolean  -- Returns `true` if blueprint
Universe:hasTag( id : integer, tag : string )  -- Returns `true` if the entity carries the given tag

Universe:queryBitmapAny( names : table )  -- Query bitmap any on this Universe

Universe:removeSystem( system : table )  -- Removes system from the collection

Universe:update( dt : number )  -- Advances the simulation by `dt` seconds
```

## `luna.scene` {#scene}

> Registers the `luna.scene.*` scene stack, registry, data store, and depth-sorter API.
>
> The scene stack manages a LIFO stack of Lua scene tables with optional
> visual transitions. Scene tables are stored via `mlua::RegistryKey` and
> identified by `SceneId`. Inter-scene data is stored similarly.

*Coverage: 28/28 items documented (100%)*

```lua
luna.scene.clear()  -- Clears the state
luna.scene.draw()  -- Draw
luna.scene.getCurrent()  -- Returns the current
luna.scene.getData( key : string )  -- Returns the data
luna.scene.getRegistered( name : string )  -- Returns the registered
luna.scene.getRegisteredNames()  -- Returns the registered names
luna.scene.getStackSize()  -- Returns the stack size
luna.scene.getTransitionProgress()  -- Returns the transition progress
luna.scene.hasData( key : string )  -- Returns true if data
luna.scene.hasRegistered( name : string )  -- Returns true if registered
luna.scene.isEmpty() -> boolean  -- Returns true if empty
luna.scene.isTransitioning()  -- Returns true if transitioning
luna.scene.newDepthSorter() -> DepthSorter  -- New depth sorter
luna.scene.pop( transition : string?, duration : number? )  -- Removes an item
luna.scene.popTo( name : string ) -> boolean  -- Removes to
luna.scene.push( scene : table, transition : string?, duration : number?, params : any? )  -- Pushes a scene onto the stack
luna.scene.registerScene( name : string, scene : table )  -- Register scene
luna.scene.removeData( key : string )  -- Removes data
luna.scene.setData( key : string, value : any )  -- Sets the data
luna.scene.switchTo( scene : table, transition : string?, duration : number?, params : any? )  -- Replaces the top scene with a new one
luna.scene.unregisterScene( name : string )  -- Unregister scene
luna.scene.update( dt : number )  -- Update
```

### `DepthSorter`

```lua
DepthSorter:add( callback : function, depth : number )  -- Registers a draw callback at the given depth layer. Higher `depth` values draw in front
DepthSorter:addObject( obj : table )  -- Registers a table object with a `draw` method at the given depth
DepthSorter:clear()  -- Removes all registered callbacks and objects without calling them
DepthSorter:flush()  -- Calls all registered draw callbacks and object `draw()` methods in sorted depth order, then clears the list
DepthSorter:getCount()  -- Returns the number of callbacks and objects currently registered
DepthSorter:sort()  -- Sorts all registered callbacks and objects by their depth values (ascending)
```

## `luna.postfx` {#postfx}

> Registers the `luna.postfx` table and exposes factory functions:
>
> - `luna.postfx.newEffect(name)` â€” creates a built-in `PostFxEffect`
>   by name (`"bloom"`, `"blur"`, `"crt"`, `"godrays"`, `"vignette"`,
>   `"colourgrade"`, `"chromatic"`).
> - `luna.postfx.newPass(shaderId)` â€” creates a `Custom` `PostFxEffect`
>   backed by an external shader resource ID.
> - `luna.postfx.newStack(width?, height?)` â€” creates an empty
>   `PostFxStack` with the given canvas dimensions.
> - `luna.postfx.newImageEffect(...)` â€” creates a per-image
>   [`LuaImageEffect`] chain (empty, single-effect, chain-table, or
>   options-table overloads).
> - `luna.postfx.loadImageEffect(path)` â€” loads a [`LuaImageEffect`]
>   chain from a TOML preset file.

*Coverage: 43/44 items documented (98%)*

```lua
luna.postfx.getEffectTypes() -> table<string>  -- Returns a Lua table listing all valid built-in effect type names
luna.postfx.loadImageEffect( path : string ) -> ImageEffect  -- Loads a per-image effect chain from a TOML preset file saved by `ImageEffect:save`
luna.postfx.newEffect( name : string ) -> PostFxEffect  -- Creates a built-in post-processing effect by name
luna.postfx.newImageEffect() -> ImageEffect  -- Creates a per-image effect chain
luna.postfx.newPass( shader_id : integer ) -> PostFxEffect  -- Creates a custom shader pass effect
luna.postfx.newStack()
```

### `ImageEffect`

```lua
ImageEffect:addEffect() -> PostFxEffect  -- Appends a new effect pass by name to the end of this effect chain
ImageEffect:clearEffects()  -- Removes all effects from this chain
ImageEffect:clone() -> ImageEffect  -- Returns a deep clone of this `ImageEffect`
ImageEffect:effectCount() -> integer  -- Returns the number of effects in this chain
ImageEffect:getEffect()  -- Returns the effect at the given 1-based index or by name, or `nil`
ImageEffect:removeEffect() -> boolean  -- Removes an effect by 1-based index or by type name
ImageEffect:save()  -- Serialises this effect chain to a TOML file at the given path
```

### `PostFxEffect`

```lua
PostFxEffect:getEffectType() -> string  -- Returns the string name of this effect's type
PostFxEffect:getParameterNames()  -- Returns a sorted list of all parameter names currently set on this effect
PostFxEffect:getType() -> string  -- Returns the string name of this effect's type
PostFxEffect:hasParameter() -> boolean  -- Returns `true` if the named parameter exists on this effect
PostFxEffect:isBuiltIn() -> boolean  -- Returns `true` if this is a built-in effect (not a custom shader pass)
PostFxEffect:isEnabled() -> boolean  -- Returns `true` if this effect is currently enabled
PostFxEffect:setBrightness()  -- Sets the colour grading brightness multiplier
PostFxEffect:setContrast()  -- Sets the colour grading contrast
PostFxEffect:setEnabled()  -- Enables or disables this effect within its parent stack
PostFxEffect:setIntensity()  -- Sets the intensity for bloom or godrays effects
PostFxEffect:setOffset()  -- Sets the chromatic aberration pixel offset
PostFxEffect:setParameter()  -- Sets a named float parameter on the effect
PostFxEffect:setRadius()  -- Sets the Gaussian blur radius
PostFxEffect:setSaturation()  -- Sets the colour grading saturation
PostFxEffect:setScanlineStrength()  -- Sets the CRT scanline strength
PostFxEffect:setStrength()  -- Sets the vignette or blur strength
PostFxEffect:setThreshold()  -- Sets the bloom bright-pass threshold
```

### `PostFxStack`

```lua
PostFxStack:add()  -- Appends a `PostFxEffect` to the end of the stack
PostFxStack:apply()  -- Applies all enabled effects in the stack
PostFxStack:beginCapture()  -- Starts a post-processing capture pass
PostFxStack:endCapture()  -- Ends a post-processing capture pass
PostFxStack:getDimensions()  -- Returns both canvas dimensions as `(width, height)`
PostFxStack:getEffect()  -- Returns the effect at a 1-based position in the chain, or `nil`
PostFxStack:getEffectCount() -> integer  -- Returns the number of effects currently in the chain (enabled and disabled)
PostFxStack:getEnabledEffects()  -- Returns a Lua table of all currently enabled effects in application order
PostFxStack:getHeight() -> integer  -- Returns the canvas height in pixels
PostFxStack:getWidth() -> integer  -- Returns the canvas width in pixels
PostFxStack:isCapturing() -> boolean  -- Returns `true` while the stack is between `beginCapture` and `endCapture`
PostFxStack:isEffectEnabled() -> boolean  -- Returns `true` if the given effect is enabled within this stack
PostFxStack:remove() -> boolean  -- Removes a `PostFxEffect` from the stack chain
PostFxStack:resize()  -- Resizes the internal canvas dimensions
```

## `luna.log` {#log}

> Structured game-level logging API (`luna.log.*`).
>
> Exposes convenience logging functions so Lua scripts can emit messages at
> specific severity levels and query or change the active log level at
> runtime without going through the `luna.system` namespace.

*Coverage: 7/7 items documented (100%)*

```lua
luna.log.debug( message : string )  -- Emit a debug-severity log message from Lua
luna.log.error( message : string )  -- Emit an error-severity log message from Lua
luna.log.getLevel()  -- Return the name of the currently active minimum log level
luna.log.info( message : string )  -- Emit an info-severity log message from Lua
luna.log.print( level : string, message : string )  -- Emit a log message from Lua at the specified severity level
luna.log.setLevel( level : string )  -- Set the minimum severity level for runtime log messages
luna.log.warn( message : string )  -- Emit a warn-severity log message from Lua
```

## `luna.debug` {#debug}

> Registers the `luna.devtools.*` runtime diagnostics and developer tools API.
>
> Five subsystems: structured logger, hierarchical profiler, frame statistics,
> file watcher, and Lua debug bridge. All module-level functions — no
> registered types.

*Coverage: 33/33 items documented (100%)*

```lua
luna.debug.clearLog()  -- Clears all log history
luna.debug.clearWatches()  -- Clears all watched paths
luna.debug.eval( code : string )  -- Evaluates a Lua string and returns success flag plus results
luna.debug.getCallStack( max_depth : integer? )  -- Returns the Lua call stack as a table of stack frames
luna.debug.getFrameHistory() -> table  -- Returns the raw frame time history
luna.debug.getFrameHistorySize() -> boolean  -- Returns the current frame history buffer size
luna.debug.getFrameStats()  -- Returns computed frame statistics
luna.debug.getLogConsole()  -- Returns whether console output is enabled
luna.debug.getLogFile() -> table  -- Returns the current log file path
luna.debug.getLogHistory( count : integer? ) -> table  -- Returns the last `count` log entries (default all)
luna.debug.getLogLevel()  -- Returns the current minimum log level
luna.debug.getProfileData( frame : integer? ) -> table  -- Returns zone data for a specific frame (0 = most recent)
luna.debug.getProfileFrameCount() -> table  -- Returns the number of retained profile frames
luna.debug.getWatchInterval()  -- Returns the current watch interval
luna.debug.getWatchedPaths() -> table  -- Returns all watched file paths
luna.debug.isConsoleOpen()  -- Returns whether the console is considered open
luna.debug.isProfilingEnabled()  -- Returns whether the profiler is enabled
luna.debug.log( level : string, message : string )  -- Log at any level with optional source/line info
luna.debug.openConsole() -> boolean  -- Opens a console window (no-op on non-Windows)
luna.debug.profileFrame()  -- Seals the current frame of profiling data
luna.debug.profilePop( name : string? )  -- Ends the most recent timing zone
luna.debug.profilePush( name : string )  -- Starts a named timing zone
luna.debug.recordFrameTime( dt_val : number )  -- Records a frame time sample
luna.debug.resetProfile()  -- Clears all profiling state
luna.debug.scan() -> table  -- Scans watched files and returns paths whose modification time changed
luna.debug.setFrameHistorySize( size : integer )  -- Sets the maximum frame history buffer size (clamped 10–10000)
luna.debug.setLogConsole( enabled : boolean )  -- Toggles stderr console output
luna.debug.setLogFile( path : string )  -- Sets the log file path. Empty string disables file logging
luna.debug.setLogLevel( level : string )  -- Sets the minimum log level to record
luna.debug.setProfilingEnabled( enabled : boolean )  -- Enables or disables the profiler
luna.debug.setWatchInterval( interval : number )  -- Sets the advisory watch interval in seconds
luna.debug.unwatch( path : string ) -> boolean  -- Removes a file from the watch list
luna.debug.watch( path : string ) -> boolean  -- Registers a file path for modification-time polling
```

## `luna.debugbridge` {#debugbridge}

> Registers the `luna.debugbridge.*` TCP debug server API.
>
> Embeds a JSON-over-TCP server (127.0.0.1 only) inside the running game.
> External tools (VS Code extension, MCP server) connect to inspect and
> control the game at runtime. Network I/O runs on a background thread;
> Lua-dependent methods are queued and dispatched via `poll()` on the main thread.

*Coverage: 15/15 items documented (100%)*

```lua
luna.debugbridge.broadcast( event : string, json_data : string )  -- Broadcasts a JSON event to all connected clients
luna.debugbridge.capturePrint( msg : string, source : string?, line : integer? )  -- Captures a print message and broadcasts it to connected clients
luna.debugbridge.clearPrintHistory()  -- Clears the print history
luna.debugbridge.getClientCount()  -- Returns the number of connected TCP clients
luna.debugbridge.getPerformance() -> table  -- Returns performance statistics
luna.debugbridge.getPort()  -- Returns the server port (0 if not running)
luna.debugbridge.getPrintHistory( count : integer? ) -> table  -- Returns the print history
luna.debugbridge.isRunning()  -- Returns whether the server is currently running
luna.debugbridge.isScreenshotRequested()  -- Returns whether a screenshot is currently requested
luna.debugbridge.poll()  -- Poll for pending Lua-dependent requests from TCP clients
luna.debugbridge.recordFrame( dt : number )  -- Records a frame time sample
luna.debugbridge.requestScreenshot( scale : integer? )  -- Flags a screenshot request for the next frame
luna.debugbridge.setMaxPrintHistory( max : integer )  -- Sets the maximum print history size
luna.debugbridge.start( port : u16? ) -> boolean  -- Start the TCP debug server on 127.0.0.1:port
luna.debugbridge.stop()  -- Stop the TCP debug server and close all connections
```

---

## `luna.docs` {#docs}

> Registers the `luna.docs.*` documentation management API.

```lua
luna.docs.checkStaleness( catalog_ud : userdata, source_dir : string ) -> table  -- Compare catalog timestamps against source files
luna.docs.coverage( catalog_ud : userdata? )  -- Coverage: (documented, total)
luna.docs.coverageModule( module_name : string, catalog_ud : userdata? )  -- Module-level coverage
luna.docs.describe( qualified_name : string, description : string )  -- Inject a description for an API entry
luna.docs.exportAll( catalog_ud : userdata, output_dir : string )  -- Export all three files to a directory
luna.docs.exportCheatsheet( catalog_ud : userdata, path : string )  -- Export one-line-per-function cheatsheet
luna.docs.exportCompletions( catalog_ud : userdata, path : string )  -- Export completions JSON for VS Code IntelliSense
luna.docs.exportHover( catalog_ud : userdata, path : string )  -- Export hover JSON
luna.docs.exportMarkdown( catalog_ud : userdata, path : string )  -- Export markdown API reference
luna.docs.exportSignatures( catalog_ud : userdata, path : string )  -- Export signatures JSON
luna.docs.getCatalog()  -- Get the current internal catalog
luna.docs.loadAll( directory : string )  -- Load all .toml files in a directory and merge
luna.docs.loadToml( path : string )  -- Load a TOML doc file into an ApiCatalog
luna.docs.quality( catalog_ud : userdata? )  -- Calculate quality metrics for a catalog
luna.docs.qualityModule( module_name : string, catalog_ud : userdata? )  -- Quality for a single module
luna.docs.resetCatalog()  -- Reset the internal catalog
luna.docs.scan( opts : table? )  -- Scan the luna.* namespace to build an API catalog from live bindings
luna.docs.scanModule( module_name : string )  -- Scan a single module's bindings
luna.docs.setParamInfo( qualified_name : string, params : table )  -- Set parameter info for an entry
luna.docs.setReturnInfo( qualified_name : string, returns : table )  -- Set return type info for an entry
luna.docs.validate( catalog_ud : userdata? )  -- Validate catalog completeness against live bindings
luna.docs.validateModule( module_name : string, catalog_ud : userdata? )  -- Validate a single module
```

## `luna.patterns` {#patterns}

> Registers the `luna.patterns.*` software design patterns API.
>
> Provides factory functions and UserData wrappers for reusable patterns:
> EventBus (prioritized pub-sub), ObjectPool (object reuse), CommandStack
> (undo/redo), ServiceLocator (DI container), Factory (named constructors),
> and SimpleState (finite state machine).

*Coverage: 47/47 items documented (100%)*

```lua
luna.patterns.newCommandStack() -> CommandStack  -- New command stack
luna.patterns.newEventBus() -> EventBus  -- New event bus
luna.patterns.newFactory() -> Factory  -- New factory
luna.patterns.newObjectPool() -> ObjectPool  -- New object pool
luna.patterns.newServiceLocator() -> ServiceLocator  -- New service locator
luna.patterns.newSimpleState() -> SimpleState  -- New simple state
```

### `CommandStack`

```lua
CommandStack:canRedo() -> integer  -- Returns `true` if redo
CommandStack:canUndo() -> boolean  -- Returns `true` if undo
CommandStack:clearAll()  -- Clear all on this CommandStack
CommandStack:execute( name : string, exec_fn : function, undo_fn : function? )  -- Execute on this CommandStack
CommandStack:getCurrentName()  -- Returns the current name
CommandStack:getHistorySize() -> integer  -- Returns the history size
CommandStack:redo() -> boolean  -- Redo on this CommandStack
CommandStack:undo() -> boolean  -- Undo on this CommandStack
```

### `EventBus`

```lua
EventBus:clear( event : string )  -- Removes all entries
EventBus:clearAll()  -- Clear all on this EventBus
EventBus:emit( args : MultiValue )  -- Emits an event
EventBus:getEvents()  -- Returns the events
EventBus:getListenerCount( event : string )  -- Returns the listener count
EventBus:off( id : integer )  -- Removes a previously registered event listener
EventBus:on( event : string, callback : function, priority : integer? )  -- Registers an event listener callback
```

> Provides Lua-level sprite animation: frame pools, named clips, speed
> control, and playback events.


### `Animation`

Lua UserData wrapper for an [`Animation`] controller.

```lua
Animation:addFrame() -> number  -- Adds a single frame to the frame pool
Animation:getClip() -> string|nil  -- Returns the name of the currently playing clip, or `nil`
Animation:getClipCount() -> number  -- Returns the total number of named clips
Animation:getFrameCount() -> number  -- Returns the total number of frames in the frame pool
Animation:getQuad() -> table|nil  -- Returns the source quad `{x, y, w, h}` for the current frame, or `nil`
Animation:getSpeed() -> number  -- Returns the playback speed multiplier
Animation:isPlaying() -> boolean  -- Returns `true` if a clip is currently playing
Animation:play() -> boolean  -- Starts playback of the named clip. Returns `true` if the clip exists
Animation:pollEvents() -> table  -- Drains and returns all pending animation events as a table
Animation:setSpeed()  -- Sets the playback speed multiplier
Animation:stop()  -- Stops playback and clears the current clip
Animation:update()  -- Advances the animation by `dt` seconds
```

## `luna.camera` {#camera}

> Registers the `luna.camera` namespace.
>
> Provides Lua-level camera management: smooth follow, dead zone,
> screen-shake, bounds clamping, and coordinate conversion via [`Camera2D`].

*Coverage: 23/23 items documented (100%)*

```lua
luna.camera.new() -> Camera2D  -- Creates a new [`Camera2D`] with the given viewport dimensions
```

### `Camera2D`

Lua UserData wrapper for a [`Camera2D`] instance.

```lua
Camera2D:clearTarget()  -- Clears the follow target (camera stops tracking)
Camera2D:getPosition() -> number  -- Returns the camera's world-space position as `x, y`
Camera2D:getRotation() -> number  -- Returns the rotation in radians
Camera2D:getViewport() -> number  -- Returns the current viewport as `x, y, w, h`
Camera2D:getVisibleArea() -> number  -- Returns the visible world area as `x, y, w, h`
Camera2D:getZoom() -> number  -- Returns the current zoom factor
Camera2D:lookAt()  -- Instantly moves the camera to look at `(x, y)`
Camera2D:move()  -- Translates the camera by `(dx, dy)` in world space
Camera2D:removeBounds()  -- Removes previously set world-space bounds
Camera2D:setBounds()  -- Sets world-space bounds for camera clamping
Camera2D:setDeadZone()  -- Sets the dead zone half-extents `(w, h)`. The camera does not move
Camera2D:setFollowSmooth()  -- Sets the follow smooth interpolation speed (`0.0` = instant snap)
Camera2D:setLookAhead()  -- Sets the look-ahead multiplier
Camera2D:setPosition()  -- Sets the camera's world-space position
Camera2D:setRotation()  -- Sets the rotation in radians
Camera2D:setTarget()  -- Sets the follow target position
Camera2D:setViewport()  -- Sets the viewport rectangle `(x, y, width, height)` in screen pixels
Camera2D:setZoom()  -- Sets the uniform zoom factor (`1.0` = natural size)
Camera2D:shake()  -- Starts a screen-shake effect
Camera2D:toScreen() -> number  -- Converts world coordinates to screen coordinates
Camera2D:toWorld() -> number  -- Converts screen coordinates to world coordinates
Camera2D:update()  -- Advances the camera simulation by `dt` seconds
```

---

## `luna.font` {#font}

> Lua API bindings for the `luna.font.*` font rasterizer module.
>
> Provides `newRasterizer`, `newTrueTypeRasterizer`, `newBMFontRasterizer`,
> and `newGlyphData` factory functions matching the LÖVE2D `love.font` module
> surface.  Font loading delegates to the same `Font::from_bytes` path used by
> `luna.graphics.newFont` so the returned `LuaFont` userdata is fully
> compatible with all existing graphics API functions.

*Coverage: 11/11 items documented (100%)*

```lua
luna.font.newBMFontRasterizer()  -- Stub: BMFont rasterization is not yet supported
luna.font.newGlyphData()  -- Returns glyph metrics for a given Unicode code point within the font
luna.font.newRasterizer()  -- Loads a TTF/OTF font file and returns a font userdata object
luna.font.newTrueTypeRasterizer()  -- Loads a TTF/OTF font with an optional hinting hint (currently ignored)
```

### `GlyphData`

```lua
GlyphData:getAdvance() -> number  -- Returns the horizontal advance width in pixels
GlyphData:getBearingX() -> number  -- Returns the horizontal bearing (cursor-to-glyph-edge offset)
GlyphData:getBearingY() -> number  -- Returns the vertical bearing (baseline-to-glyph-top offset)
GlyphData:getGlyph() -> integer  -- Returns the Unicode code point as an integer
GlyphData:getGlyphString() -> string  -- Returns the glyph character as a UTF-8 string
GlyphData:getHeight() -> integer  -- Returns the pixel height of the glyph bitmap
GlyphData:getWidth() -> integer  -- Returns the pixel width of the glyph bitmap
```

## `luna.network` {#network}

> Registers the `luna.network` namespace.
>
> Provides Lua-level UDP networking via ENet: host creation, peer
> management, packet sending and receiving. Wraps [`NetworkHost`].

*Coverage: 5/5 items documented (100%)*

```lua
luna.network.newHost() -> NetworkHost  -- Creates a new [`NetworkHost`] bound to the given address
```

### `NetworkHost`

Lua UserData wrapper for a [`NetworkHost`].

```lua
NetworkHost:disconnect()  -- Gracefully disconnects a peer. The disconnect event arrives on the next `service()`
NetworkHost:flush()  -- Flushes pending sends immediately
NetworkHost:getAddress() -> string  -- Returns the local address of this host as `"ip:port"`
NetworkHost:service() -> table|nil  -- Polls the network for one event. Returns an event table or `nil`
```

---

## `luna.overlay` {#overlay}

> Registers the `luna.overlay` table and exposes a single factory:
>
> - `luna.overlay.newOverlay(width?, height?)` â€” creates a `LuaOverlay`
>   UserData object.

*Coverage: 56/57 items documented (98%)*

```lua
luna.overlay.newOverlay()
```

### `Overlay`

```lua
Overlay:clear()  -- Resets all overlay subsystems to their inactive defaults
Overlay:draw()  -- Placeholder for draw â€” actual rendering handled by the game loop
Overlay:getAmbientColor() -> number  -- Returns the current ambient tint colour as `(r, g, b, a)`
Overlay:getCloudCount() -> number  -- Returns the current cloud shadow blob count
Overlay:getCloudOpacity() -> number  -- Returns the cloud shadow opacity (0.0â€“1.0)
Overlay:getCloudScale() -> number  -- Returns the cloud shadow blob size scale
Overlay:getCloudSpeed() -> number  -- Returns the cloud shadow scroll speed in pixels per second
Overlay:getDimensions() -> number  -- Returns both canvas dimensions as `(width, height)`
Overlay:getFilmGrainIntensity() -> number  -- Returns the current film grain noise amplitude (0.0â€“1.0)
```

## `luna.pipeline` {#pipeline}

> Registers the `luna.pipeline.*` DAG pipeline orchestrator API.
>
> Exposes `LuaStep` and `LuaPipeline` UserData types wrapping `crate::pipeline`
> with Lua callback dispatch for step execution.

*Coverage: 50/53 items documented (94%)*

```lua
luna.pipeline.fromTable()
luna.pipeline.newPipeline()
luna.pipeline.newStep()
```

### `Pipeline`

```lua
Pipeline:addStep() -> Pipeline  -- Adds a step to the pipeline. Returns self for chaining
Pipeline:cancel()  -- Cancels all pending and waiting steps
Pipeline:clear()  -- Clears all steps from the pipeline
Pipeline:getContext()  -- Returns the stored async context table, or nil if not in an async run
Pipeline:getErrorMode() -> String  -- Returns the current error mode as a string (`"abort"` or `"continue"`)
Pipeline:getExecutionOrder()  -- Returns the topological execution order as an array of step names
Pipeline:getName() -> String  -- Returns the pipeline's name
Pipeline:getParallelGroups()  -- Returns parallel execution groups as a nested array. Returns `(nil, error_string)` on cycle
Pipeline:getResult()  -- Returns the current result table built from step states, or nil if nothing has run
Pipeline:getStep()  -- Returns the LuaStep wrapper for the named step, or nil
Pipeline:getStepCount() -> usize  -- Returns the total number of steps
Pipeline:getSteps() -> table  -- Returns a Lua array of all step wrappers in the pipeline
Pipeline:getStepsByTag() -> table  -- Returns a Lua array of all steps whose tag matches the given string
Pipeline:isComplete() -> bool  -- Returns `true` if all steps have reached a terminal state
Pipeline:isRunning() -> bool  -- Returns `true` if the pipeline is currently running asynchronously
Pipeline:removeStep()  -- Removes a step from the pipeline by name
Pipeline:reset()  -- Resets all step states and clears the async context
Pipeline:run() -> table  -- Executes the pipeline synchronously in topological order
Pipeline:runAsync()  -- Starts an async pipeline run. Steps are executed one-per-frame via `update(dt)`
Pipeline:setErrorMode()  -- Sets the pipeline error mode: `"abort"` or `"continue"`
Pipeline:setName()  -- Sets the pipeline's name
Pipeline:setOnComplete()  -- Sets the callback to invoke when the pipeline completes
Pipeline:setOnStepError()  -- Sets the callback to invoke each time a step fails
Pipeline:toTable() -> table  -- Serialises the pipeline definition to a Lua table (no callbacks)
Pipeline:update() -> bool  -- Advances the async pipeline by one tick. Returns `true` when all steps are done
Pipeline:validate()  -- Validates the pipeline DAG. Returns `(ok, error_array)`
```

### `PipelineStep`

```lua
PipelineStep:dependsOn() -> PipelineStep  -- Adds a dependency on another step. Returns self for chaining
PipelineStep:getAttempt() -> u32  -- Returns the number of execution attempts so far
PipelineStep:getData()  -- Retrieves a metadata value by key, returning nil if not found
PipelineStep:getDelay() -> f32  -- Returns the configured delay in seconds
PipelineStep:getDependencies() -> table  -- Returns the list of dependency step names
PipelineStep:getDependencyCount() -> usize  -- Returns the number of declared dependencies
PipelineStep:getDuration() -> f32  -- Returns total seconds spent executing this step
PipelineStep:getError()  -- Returns the error message from the last failed attempt, or nil
PipelineStep:getName() -> String  -- Returns the unique name of this step
PipelineStep:getRetryCount() -> u32  -- Returns the configured retry count
PipelineStep:getStatus() -> String  -- Returns the current execution status as a string
PipelineStep:getTag()  -- Returns the tag on this step, or nil if unset
PipelineStep:getTimeout() -> f32  -- Returns the timeout stored in metadata, or 0.0 if unset
PipelineStep:isOptional() -> bool  -- Returns whether this step is marked as optional
PipelineStep:setCallback()  -- Stores a Lua function as the execute callback for this step
PipelineStep:setCondition()  -- Stores a Lua function (or nil) as the run-condition for this step
PipelineStep:setData()  -- Stores an arbitrary string value under the given key in step metadata
PipelineStep:setDelay()  -- Sets the delay (in seconds) to wait after dependencies finish
PipelineStep:setOnError()  -- Stores a Lua function (or nil) to call if this step fails
PipelineStep:setOptional()  -- Marks whether this step is optional (downstream steps continue on failure)
PipelineStep:setRetryCount()  -- Sets the maximum number of retry attempts on failure
PipelineStep:setRetryDelay()  -- Sets the delay (in seconds) between retry attempts
PipelineStep:setTag()  -- Sets the tag on this step for grouping and filtering
PipelineStep:setTimeout()  -- Stores a timeout (in seconds) in the step's metadata
```

## `luna.procgen` {#procgen}

> Registers the `luna.procgen` namespace.
>
> Exposes stateless procedural generation utilities: cellular-automata
> cave maps, BFS flood fill, Poisson disk sampling, periodic Perlin noise,
> and Voronoi diagrams.

*Coverage: 5/5 items documented (100%)*

```lua
luna.procgen.cellularAutomata() -> table  -- Generates a cave-like map using cellular automata
luna.procgen.floodFill() -> table  -- BFS flood fill on a flat grid of bytes
luna.procgen.perlinNoise() -> number  -- Evaluates a single periodic Perlin noise value at `(x, y)`
luna.procgen.poissonDisk() -> table  -- Generates Poisson disk sample points using Bridson's algorithm
luna.procgen.voronoi() -> table  -- Generates a Voronoi diagram for a set of seed points
```

## `luna.serial` {#serial}

> Registers the `luna.serial.*` format serialization API.
>
> Provides `luna.serial` with functions to parse and serialize JSON, TOML, and CSV
> to/from Lua tables. YAML removed — use TOML for config (design-assumption B-05).
> No file I/O — strings in, strings out.

*Coverage: 6/6 items documented (100%)*

```lua
luna.serial.fromCsv( s : string, delimiter : string?, has_headers : boolean? )  -- Parses a CSV string and returns a Lua table (sequence of row tables)
luna.serial.fromJson( s : string )  -- Parses a JSON string and returns a Lua table
luna.serial.fromToml( s : string )  -- Parses a TOML string and returns a Lua table
luna.serial.toCsv( table : table, delimiter : string?, has_headers : boolean? )  -- Serializes a Lua table (sequence of row tables) to a CSV string
luna.serial.toJson( table : table, pretty : boolean? )  -- Serializes a Lua table to a JSON string
luna.serial.toToml( table : table )  -- Serializes a Lua table to a TOML string
```

## `luna.terminal` {#terminal}

> Lua API bindings for the `luna.terminal.*` text-mode terminal emulator.
>
> This binding layer uses userdata handles so terminal methods work with
> idiomatic Lua colon syntax. Widget constructors return detached widget
> handles that carry their own data and callbacks until `addWidget()`
> attaches them to a terminal.
